word: G4363 - προσπίπτω - prospíptō - pros-pip'-to - Beat upon

sections.paragraph:
- Original: προσπίπτω

- Transliteration: Prospipto

- Phonetic: pros-pip'-to

- Definition:

1. to fall forwards, fall down, prostrate one's self before, in homage or supplication: at one's feet

2. to rush upon, beat against

a. of winds beating upon a house

- Origin: from G4314 and G4098

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From G4314 and G4098; to fall towards that is (gently) prostrate oneself (in supplication or homage) or (violently) to rush upon (in storm): - beat upon fall (down) at (before).
